story.text: *Coordination Across Difference — 39 proverbs, one line carried from each spell.*

> How do sovereign beings coordinate without collapsing into the uniformity they escaped? Through ⿻—overlap without merger, connection without collapse, many in relationship. > — Plurality Grimoire > Technology and democracy became enemies through bad choices, not nature. Taiwan proves they can be allies again. > — The Problem and the Proof > You are not broken. You are ⿻. > — The First Overlap > The gulf between technology and democracy opened through three bad choices. > — The Widening Gulf > Where tectonic plates collide, mountains rise. > — A View from Yushan > The difference is not better people. The difference is better architecture. > — The Life of a Digital Democracy > ⿻ is not an ideology imposed on the world. It is recognition of how reality is already structured. > — What is ⿻? > The fish does not discover water. The human can discover plurality. > — The Glyph That Breathes > The spider does not command the web. The spider creates the conditions. > — The Web Beneath the Web > The architect studies those who built before them. > — The Teachers Before the Drake > The pioneer clears the path; the settler walks it; the forgetter loses it; the rememberer finds it again. > — The Path Abandoned > Before the application, the operating system. Five pillars support plurality. > — Rights as Infrastructure > The carpenter who neglects the foundation builds a house that will fall. > — The Foundation Beneath the Floor > The slave is named by the master. The free person names themselves. > — The Name You Give Yourself > One stick breaks easily. A bundle of sticks is strong. But the bundle must bind itself. > — The Guilds That Form Themselves > The merchant who records every purchase owns more than goods—they own the customer. > — The Market That Doesn't Devour > The miser owns alone and guards anxiously. The community owns together and grows abundantly. > — What Can Be Held Together > A feast behind a locked door is not generosity. A democracy behind a paywall is not democracy. > — The Door That Opens Both Ways > Seven collaborative technologies run on the foundation of rights. > — Collaborative Technology > The wise teacher does not pour knowledge into the student. > — Words Before Words > The house built by one is owned by one. The village built by many belongs to many. > — The World We Build Together > The tree does not boast of its own growth. > — The Creation That Compounds > The wise leader does not speak loudest but listens longest. > — The Hearing at Scale > The bamboo survives the storm by bending. > — Rules That Learn > The scale that weighs a feather and a stone equally is not fair—it is blind. > — The Weight of Wanting > The field that remembers what was planted grows richer each season. > — The Market That Remembers > The single circle includes or excludes. The many overlapping circles create something none could create alone. > — The Circle That Includes > Plurality must land in the domains where life actually happens. > — Impact > The worker who owns nothing is owned. > — The Forge of Peers > The body speaks only to the one who inhabits it. > — The Body's Secrets > The town crier speaks to everyone. The algorithm speaks to each person differently. > — The Signal and the Noise > The forest does not negotiate with the axe. > — The Commons That Breathes > The seed knows how to grow. The gardener creates conditions. > — The Mind That Grows > Technology alone cannot create plurality. Policy enables or prevents. > — From Vision to World > The gardener cannot force the plant to grow. > — The Laws That Enable > The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now. > — The Window That Closes > The ceremony that completes is the ceremony that releases. Go forth. Be plural. > — The Ceremony Completes > Protection enables exit enables coordination. > — Plurality Grimoire > See the overlap, cross the gulf, climb the mountain, practice the democracy, breathe the glyph, weave the web, learn the teachers, remember the pioneers, lay the foundation, claim your name, form the guild, shield the market, share the commons, open the door, speak before words, build the world, create together, hear at scale, learn the rules, weigh the wanting, remember the market, include the circle, forge the peers, guard the body, find the signal, breathe the commons, grow the mind, enable the laws, close the window, complete the ceremony—sovereignty emerges from coordination. > — Plurality Grimoire
